name: Vasopressin
executiveSummary: For hypotension refractory to catecholamines with hypovolemia.
indications:
## Main indications

For hypotension refractory to catecholamines with hypovolemia.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion per institutional presentation.
adulto.reconstitution: Reconstitute according to prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
adulto.finalConcentration: Concentration according to service guide (infusion pump).
adulto.dose: 0.03–0.04 IU/min fixed or 0.01–0.04 IU/min according to sepsis protocol.
adulto.infusionRate: Titrate according to BP and perfusion
adulto.administration: Continuous IV on pump; central route preferred.
adulto.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
adulto.notes: Monitor HR, invasive BP, diuresis and peripheral perfusion.

pediatrico.reconstitution: Reconstitute according to prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
pediatrico.finalConcentration: Concentration according to service guide (infusion pump).
pediatrico.infusionRate: Titrate according to BP and perfusion
pediatrico.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
pediatrico.notes: For IV administration, dilute in normal saline or 5% dextrose before use, maximum administration concentration 1 U/mL. Discard unused diluted solution after 18 h at room temperature and 24 h under refrigeration.

neonatal.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion per institutional presentation.
neonatal.reconstitution: Reconstitute according to prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
neonatal.finalConcentration: Concentration according to service guide (infusion pump).
neonatal.dose: Per kg dose according to NICU protocol for refractory shock.
neonatal.infusionRate: Titrate according to BP and perfusion
neonatal.administration: Continuous IV on pump; central route preferred.
neonatal.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
neonatal.notes: Monitor HR, invasive BP, diuresis and peripheral perfusion.

stability:
## Stability

- Dilution stable according to prescribing information; rotate infusion site.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Hypertension, bradycardia, arrhythmias, venous thrombosis, fever, vertigo, tremor, diaphoresis.
